customer_anxiety_profile: David is not anxious about solar — he's genuinely curious but life-constrained. His wife's stroke and homebound status, his own business wind-down, and a busy near-term schedule are real friction sources, not deflections. He's polite and self-deprecating, which can mask how much the gas-primary concern actually deflated his interest.
unresolved_concerns: Gas is the primary energy source in the home — David directly questioned whether solar addresses his actual energy cost. This was never resolved with a value bridge.
David does not know his average PGE bill — the electric usage baseline is unknown and the bill was never captured.
No locked appointment date or time — the callback is fully unanchored and dependent on David sending a bill photo he may not prioritize.
sensitive_topics_raised: Wife's stroke — she is homebound and her condition limits David's scheduling flexibility. She is also on title and was never included in any appointment anchor.
David is semi-retired, running a business, and managing a building sale — he described himself as 'not making a lot of money' and uncertain about taxable income implications for the incentive.
Wife recently stopped working due to stroke — household income has changed; tax credit eligibility may be more complex than the rep's yes/no framing suggested.
exact_promises_rep_made: Rep will send an email to davidd@had-law.com asking David to reply with a photo of his PGE bill (bar graph showing usage history).
Rep will reach out again once the bill comes through to find a time for a specialist to connect.
closer_should_know: David's son already has solar on his North Portland home and David cited his son's low bill as his primary motivation — this is pre-built social proof from a trusted source. Open with it. Also: David's wife is on title, is homebound due to a stroke, and was never included in any appointment anchor. The closer must include her from the first contact. The gas-primary concern is real and unresolved — address it directly before presenting the electric design.
